method_atom: an interactive WYSIWYG graphical editor that lets a user construct structured node-and-connector diagrams entirely through mouse click-and-drag operations without programming, and that serializes the resulting layout to a portable markup file for later reuse, exports it to a vector graphics format, and auto-generates equivalent typesetting/markup source code from the visual representation.
object_atom: Feynman diagrams, the graphical representations of perturbative particle-physics interactions, together with the LaTeX code used to typeset them.
brief_reason: The transferable part is the WYSIWYG click-and-drag diagram-editing, serialization, vector export, and code-generation machinery; the Feynman-diagram subject and its LaTeX output are the physics-specific object.
